title: parkia javanica
intro: Parkia javanica, also known as the Javanese walnut or keluak, is a plant species in the legume family native to Southeast Asia. The seeds of the plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect against diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The health benefits of Parkia javanica include reducing inflammation, improving blood sugar control, and lowering cholesterol levels, making it a valuable component in herbal preparations for various health conditions. The seeds are often prepared as a traditional medicine by boiling or roasting them, and they can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
